programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A bomb detonates at a military charity concert, killing two men. Meanwhile, Jimmy prepares for parenthood. Keith Carradine guest stars, with Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gibbs joins forces with the FBI when the body of a congressman and former agent washes ashore in New Orleans. Together they try to determine if the murder could be linked to a well-known case the victim had previously worked on during his career. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. DiNozzo, McGee and the FBI continue to track political connections to a case, and Bishop sends DiNozzo a souvenir. Meanwhile, Gibbs and Special Agent Pride chase leads in New Orleans after evidence points to a copycat killer. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Pauley Perrette and Scott Bakula

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee's girlfriend Delilah makes a breakthrough in a case involving the alleged death of a US navy lieutentant. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team becomes involved in an international incident when the body of an American petty officer is found by an estate agent in a bungalow that has been repossessed. Guest starring Tracy Middendorf, Daniel Gillies and Kristen Ariza

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when two pharmaceutical executives are murdered, uncovering a criminal who changes his identity for each offence he commits, and has evaded capture across several countries. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The American crime drama returns with the team still reeling from the Department of Defence investigation, with Ziva, McGee and Tony having handed in their badges. Parsons continues his inquiries into Gibbs and his agents, but events take a turn when an explosion kills the Secretary of the US Navy and seriously injures the director of Homeland Security. When attempts are made on the lives of Gibbs and Tony, it appears that all of the NCIS agents are in mortal danger. Starring Mark Harmon and Colin Hanks

programme.desc: Smithy puts on a brave face for his court appearance but CID's efforts to link Larson with Louise's death are derailed when their one lead - a gang member held on drugs charges - slips through the net. Ramani De Costa loses her appetite for community safety work after successfully investigating the case of a girl injured in an Angolan ritual exorcism, and Terry's romantic hopes receive a setback

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry's romantic ambitions get a boost when Ramani ends her relationship with Saul, but she's busy impressing DCI Caddick of the Child Abuse Investigation Team with her ability to build links with the immigrant community. Together they discover a missing girl is a victim of trafficking. Meanwhile, Cindy Hunter's desperate desire to adopt a child brings her marriage with Phil to breaking point

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Zain mounts a personal crusade to get Smithy off the hook and prove he was framed by Pete Larson, but DCI Keane of the DPS takes exception to his maverick methods and throws him off the case at a crucial moment. Ramani and Terry investigate a claim of child abuse 20 years after the event, and their sensitive handling of a tough investigation earns Ramani a job offer from DCI Caddick

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tough tactics and a high-risk operation pay off when the rival gang leaders are arrested on their way to a showdown. Having helped forestall a major outbreak of violence, Lewis feels alienated from his own community and decides to throw in his lot with the police, despite a suspicion of racism among his colleagues. Leela, Honey and Emma question a timid husband whose cheating wife has disappeared

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Change is on the way to the sleepy market town of Cranford as the railway pushes out from Manchester, but many of the female residents are more excited about the arrival of the handsome new doctor, Frank Harrison. Adapted by Heidi Thomas, with an all-star cast including Judi Dench and Eileen Atkins, who was awarded a Bafta for her role as the authoritative Miss Deborah Jenkyns

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: The town's residents prepare for Lady Ludlow's garden party, but the occasion is marred by rumours the railway line will come right into Cranford, leaving Deborah feeling betrayed. Dr Harrison's plans to become better acquainted with Sophy are thwarted by Caroline Tomkinson and Clara Smith, Mr Carter takes pity on an illiterate 10-year-old and Miss Matty is shaken to see a face from her past. Starring Judi Dench, Michael Gambon and Eileen Atkins

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Christmas arrives in the town, but Lady Ludlow regrets any notion of seasonal charity when Mr Carter asks her to intervene in the case of Job Gregson, who has been blamed for a spate of thefts. Miss Matty is delighted when Mr Holbrook invites her to his farm, and Dr Harrison's friend Jack sends Caroline Tomkinson a card hinting she should expect a proposal from the medic. Starring Judi Dench, Michael Gambon and Philip Glenister

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Miss Matty confides in Mary about Mr Holbrook's death, prompting her to inform Major Gordon of Jessie's regret over not marrying him. Dr Harrison tells Rev Hutton of his intentions toward Sophy and asks permission to court her, blissfully unaware that the generosity he has shown to Mrs Rose has accidentally created an unlikely love triangle. Penultimate episode of the costume drama, starring Judi Dench and Simon Woods

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: The true nature of friendship within Cranford is revealed as the ladies rally to help Miss Matty when she receives terrible news. Meanwhile, Dr Harrison suffers as his career, reputation and relationship all seem ruined, while Miss Galindo and Mr Carter grow closer as they face up to the tragedy of Hanbury. Judi Dench, Philip Glenister and Simon Woods star

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Having finally been convinced of Regina's duplicity, George warns Elizabeth to be careful of giving the game away. James takes Harry to a private party in Sydney, but Henry is furious. Roy continues to act as Sarah's protector and is on hand when Gordon Walsh comes calling

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah is warned by Carolyn that Regina has escaped her intended incarceration in an asylum and to be on guard, but Sarah believes her to be long gone. Elizabeth is exasperated with Rose who refuses to divulge her mistresses whereabouts

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth agrees to meet the Ladies' Luncheon Club's VIP at the railway station - but the assignment proves more difficult than imagined. Nigel Davenport guest stars

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth continues her search for a small country retreat - but her idea of small is not quite the same as everybody else's. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth and Richard finally move into their new country retreat, and while she thinks it is 'olde worlde bijou', he finds it a decided disappointment. Suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Veteran rock band the Flowers of Progress are on the island to record a new album after years of being apart, and with a major sponsorship deal hanging in the balance, tensions are running high. However, their reunion proves to be short-lived when lead singer Stevie Smith is electrocuted in the swimming pool. It seems the victim had a stormy relationship with his brother and fellow band member Jim, while there are rifts between the others and more than one has an axe to grind. But how could any of them have done the deed when they were all in the studio at the time? To add to Humphrey's problems, he is still finding his feet without Camille, although the arrival of new officer JP helps lighten Dwayne's workload. Neil Morrissey and Sally Phillips guest star

programme.title: My Life Is Murder
programme.desc: A man is found shot dead in a motel room. The door is found to have been chain-locked from the inside, so Alexa is left to ponder how the killer managed to escape. Detective thriller, starring Lucy Lawless and Bernard Curry

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Newly promoted DI Reid leads the murder investigation when a busker is stabbed to death down a dark alley in Glasgow city centre. Vital evidence turns up in the hands of two art students who were photographing the area at the time, but as the case progresses, Ross's personal life begins to unravel. Detective drama, starring Blythe Duff, John Michie and Alex Norton, with Siobhan Redmond, Niall Greig Fulton and Steven Pacey

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: As Josh and Colette wait to see if Natalie will pull through, their wedding seems farther away than ever and the kindly paramedic begins to wonder whether they are fated never to be together. What the couple don't know is that their colleagues are plotting on their behalf, and when Natalie shows signs of improvement it seems they'll finally reach the altar _ but there's yet another hitch when it turns out that 'Kev the Rev' isn't a real vicar at all. Unfortunately, Patrick's in no mood for celebrating when an argument with lover Rachel prompts her to storm off, and before he gets a chance to apologise he hears she has been brought into hospital after being seriously injured in an attack. While trying to console her distraught husband Greg, he learns she is carrying his child - setting the scene for a tragic climax. Ian Bleasdale, Adjoa Andoh, Ian Kelsey and Amy Robbins star, with a guest appearance by Sylvester McCoy

Unfortunately, the upheaval puts her on a collision course with senior partner Will, who already has his hands full helping a teenager living rough in Cardale. programme.desc: The British send their finest French-speaking agent to say 'good moaning' to Rene and his colleagues and, dressed as a pantomime cow, the two airmen narrowly avoid being milked before going to a landing field to pick up their plane home

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo spots a sinister looking motorcyclist, and after recovering from the fright eventually concludes it could be one of his old girlfriends. Long-running comedy, starring Bill Owen

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: Bacchus and Gently are called in to investigate the death of a miner, which is being treated as suspicious because even though his body was discovered down the pit, records show he was above ground when he disappeared. Alleged victim Arthur Hawkes was a proud union man, and his children confirm he fought long and hard to keep the closure-threatened mine open. But as the detectives dig deeper, they find that not everyone agrees with this portrait of him. Could he really have sold his union comrades down the river? Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby star, with Simon Greenall, Jack Deam and Michael Socha

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team is asked to reinvestigate the 1975 death of a union leader, whose body was found in the Thames. As the detectives delve deeper into the case, a financial scandal involving misappropriated funds is uncovered and a Labour peer points the finger of suspicion at the victim's deputy, but Brian's increasingly erratic behaviour threatens to hamper the inquiry. Frances Barber, Kenneth Cranham and Dave Hill guest star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Leo and Harry are called to investigate the unexpected deaths of three patients on the same ward at a London hospital, and are introduced to DC Julia Catina and DI Frank Skipper. Suspicion falls on a professor, but when he commits suicide, Nikki comes under pressure from his daughter to find a reason for his actions, and the pathologist soon makes a link to a case involving rape and murder

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: Sequel to The White Queen, set in the Middle Ages and picking up as the tumultuous War of the Roses comes to an end, a new generation ascends to the throne in a tale of power, political clashes, family, love and betrayal. As the victor of the Battle of Bosworth, Henry Tudor, is set to ascend the throne and Elizabeth of York and her family, supporters of the former King, find themselves in grave danger. Lizzie must fulfil her promise to marry King Henry VII and bring peace to the nation. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: King Henry VII sets out to establish himself as the new sovereign, but finds a dangerous, deeply divided kingdom. Lizzie's loyalties are further complicated by the child she is expecting. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Will upsets Erica by advertising for a new partner without consulting her. Unfortunately, he only has a short time to mull the matter over before he clashes with Andrew about the welfare of a 12-year-old boy trapped in a bitter custody battle _ and as if that wasn't enough to contend with, practice nurse Laura is run off the road by a fellow driver. Drama, starring Simon Shepherd, Gary Mavers and Saskia Wickham

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Part one of two. Paul's first family holiday in six years turns out to be more business than pleasure when a suspected suicide arouses his suspicions. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon's growing relationship with boyfriend Colin hits the rocks, threatening her new-found happiness. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Diana tries her hand at being nice for a 'glamorous granny' competition at Bayview. Stephanie Cole stars

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: While driving a borrowed armoured car, Rene and Captain Geering are mistaken for an ammunition convoy and ambushed by the Resistance, giving Monsieur Alfonse the ideal opportunity to woo Edith. Comedy, starring Gorden Kaye

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Alcohol threatens to prevent Yorkshire's first trainee astronaut from doing his job, so Foggy prepares to step into his boots. Brian Wilde stars

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A prostitute turns up at the station claiming to have been raped, and as Gently looks back at past cases, he is shocked to discover that very few have been successfully prosecuted - if they made it to court in the first place. Risking animosity from his colleagues, he becomes suspicious when he notices the same police inspector's name appearing repeatedly in the files. To complicate matters, the body of another rape victim is found, prompting a visit to the local brothel. Meanwhile, Bacchus has started an affair with Gemma, the wife of a fellow copper. Martin Shaw, Lee Ingleby and Lisa McGrillis star, with guest appearances from Denise Welch, Derek Riddell and Emily Woof

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A dog is found killed in a manner that matches a series of animal slayings from 30 years earlier. The team reopens the case, but the cruelty of the crimes affects Brian's judgement - especially when his own pet dies. Jack's deteriorating health causes concern for his colleagues and a visit to a museum leads to an alarming discovery. Richard Briers guest stars

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A preserved female body is found buried in a Sheffield peat moor, and DI Sonia Hardwick calls in archaeologist Patrick Cain to help with the investigation, assisted by Nikki. When an examination of the corpse suggests a sexually motivated attack, Leo suspects there is a link to a serial killer case he dealt with in 1985, but following the lead means returning to his home town and confronting the memories of his dead wife and child. Guest starring Mark Benton and Barbara Flynn, with William Gaminara

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: Elizabeth struggles through her labour, while King Henry's envoys embark on a diplomatic mission to Burgundy, a York stronghold abroad. Negotiations are jeopardised by a twist of fate, and Lizzie learns she has more in common with her husband than she first imagined. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy and Michelle Fairley star

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: The York princesses are strategically married off to strengthen the Tudor cause, but further political challenges are on the horizon. When King Henry finds himself back on the battlefield to protect his reign, Lizzie learns that the threats they face may be closer to home. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star

programme.desc: Arsenal v Manchester City. Action from the opening semi-final at Wembley Stadium, where the teams battled for a place in the final. programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from a regional perspective

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Charles Hanson are in Oswestry, Shropshire, to help two teams look for items, while host Natasha Raskin Sharp has advice on collectibles from occupied Japan

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley finds out about the vicars of Eyam, Derbyshire, who initiated a lockdown 355 years ago to protect the rest of northern England from the bubonic plague

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore returns to transform more items reclaimed from a tip, beginning by rescuing a granite worktop from Altrincham Recycling Centre in Greater Manchester

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: The third part of a look back at the Games, focusing on the second half of the Games - a hectic eight days in which the British gold rush continued in east London

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin goes property hunting with a couple of new grandparents who have £800,000 to buy a home in Wiltshire close to their family. The presenter also takes time out to learn about the traditional process of curing Wiltshire bacon

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Tom Heap are in the Shropshire Hills to find out about an ambitious project to create a nature corridor between the two peaks of Long Mynd and Stiperstones. Tom meets a farmer who's become a convert to environmentally friendly regenerative farming, while Ellie goes in search of rare and much misunderstood wildlife. Charlotte Smith tests the strength of Britain's food supply system in the wake of the pandemic and with Brexit on the horizon

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manchester United v Chelsea (Kick-off 6.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the last-four match at Wembley Stadium. The sides last met here in the final of this competition two years ago, when Chelsea prevailed 1-0 courtesy of an Eden Hazard penalty. However, United are unbeaten in the six meetings between the clubs in all competitions and have defeated the Blues three times this season, completing a double over them in the league and winning at Stamford Bridge in the fourth round of the Carabao Cup

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the historic Buckfast Abbey in Devon, where experts examine an 18th-century tankard, an iron chest with a hidden lock dating back to the 1640s, London street signs, a Biba frock, and a piece of shrapnel from the battleship Bismarck

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Anna is tried for murder, but Emery is still missing - along with her alibi - and Moody refuses to act on her behalf as he believes the case to be indefensible. However, due to a mix-up the lawyer ends up with Lauderback's trunk and discovers a series of letters from Crosbie that reveal a startling fact. Lydia hosts a seance to make contact with Emery, intending to implicate Anna further, but instead she channels the spirit of Sook and inadvertently accuses Carver of murder. Period drama, starring Eva Green

programme.title: Match of the Day 2

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development of a semi in Mottingham in south-east London, a detached in Littleover, Derby and a terrace in Dover in Kent

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles introduces footage of near misses caught on CCTV, including a couple out walking along the pavement who were hit by an out-of-control car

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Steph Connell and Thomas Forrester help teams look for items at Nottingham racecourse, while presenter Anita Manning gets to the bottom of a local UFO sighting

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Death reunites the residents once again, but some relationships are beyond repair, while Sabine pulls an outrageous stunt. Australian drama, starring Bridie McKim

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps an Anglo-Icelandic couple, who need to relocate from Reykjavik to Aberdeenshire on their £380,000 budget before their daughter starts university in the region

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A fibre artist, a ceramicist and an abstract painter create artworks for a Staffordshire who woman wants a piece to mark the birth of her first grandchild. Nick Knowles presents

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: With the help of archive, Jay Blades charts how three inventions fixed in the barn - a magic lantern, projector and mutoscope - helped influence modern TV and film technology

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The World Health Organisation urged countries to stamp out infections as soon as they developed, but the UK government's initial scientific advice said the route out of the crisis was for most of us to catch the virus so we could develop 'herd immunity'. Dr Faye Kirkland investigates this approach to the Covid-19 pandemic, asking whether it was a dangerous gamble with people's lives or a sound scientific method. Faced with a growing backlash and warnings that the NHS was close to being overwhelmed, politicians denied herd immunity was the policy and within a few days Britain pivoted to a new strategy - lockdown

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Diane Parish and Tameka Empson, aka sisters Denise and Kim Fox, for a socially-distanced reunion as Tameka returns to set for the first time since 2019. The pair reveal all about their close working relationship and tell the stories behind some of their biggest moments - from Tameka reliving her infamous live labour scenes for the show's 30th anniversary to Diane explaining why she once had to pretend to all her friends and family that she'd quit the soap

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Cash-strapped Alison and Mike think all their problems have been solved when they inherit a grand country house, unaware that it is falling apart and teeming with the ghosts of former inhabitants. The spooks must act immediately to halt the new owners' plans to turn the house into a hotel, but their attempts to scare them away fail miserably. Comedy, starring Mathew Baynton, Simon Farnaby, Martha Howe-Douglas, Jim Howick, Laurence Rickard, Ben Willbond, Charlotte Ritchie and Kiell Smith-Bynoe

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: Agatha and Hayden attend an emotional vigil for missing baby Ben, while Megan and Jack's relationship is deteriorating under the strain. Agatha begins to panic when she realises that 'Rory' is becoming seriously unwell, but she is unwilling to seek medical help.

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: Simon brings in a friend to try to convince his two stars to put their faith in him, while Michael worries about a neighbour when he spots an ambulance outside next door. David not only manages to keep the house in order and the home-schooling ticking over in Georgia's absence, but also gets around to finishing a draft of his screenplay

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: The two business partners head out on a double date, with Kazim the one in love and Jeremiah his reluctant wingman. Out of their comfort zone, they head to the East End to meet the girls, but what follows is a very strange dinner - which goes from bad to worse very quickly. Return of the comedy, starring Kayode Ewumi and Trieve Blackwood-Cambridge

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Deborah Cox hosts as the contestants do battle in a song-and-dance diss-track showdown, with judges Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, Mo Gilligan and Anne-Marie pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, and Jamie Redknapp takes on Dina Asher-Smith in The Sporting Fix. Plus, Zara Larsson performs in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Chris Bavin and Gloria Hunniford are joined by Edwina Currie as they examine conflicting media stories about the safety of eating eggs, 30 years on from the salmonella scandal

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large terraced house in Earlsfield in London, ancient woodland in Eythorne in Kent, and a building housing six flats in Tonypandy in Glamorgan are all sold at auction

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A Coastguard crew fights to save a film extra who is taken ill at the top of a cathedral tower, and customers at a pub run from an out-of-control lorry. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ochuko Ojiri and Jonathan Pratt help two teams scour an antiques fair at the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthenshire, while presenter Eric Knowles visits a rugby union club to see a collection of sporting footwear memorabilia

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Time together is revealing for Ana and Ryan, and also for Renee and Mark. Kam faces a tough interview

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin meets a couple of creative Californians keen to make a lifestyle leap to the Welsh borders, and also discovers how llamas are thriving in the British countryside

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges an impressionist painter, a metal sculptor and an artist who paints in a caravan to create artwork for a London couple who have just renovated their home

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades revisits three fixes from the barn - a polyphon, gramophone and transistor radio - to tell the story of how modern music has been influenced by vintage music players

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: In an episode originally broadcast on 27 October 1998, Pat and Peggy clash spectacularly following an accusation that Frank and Pat are having an affair behind their partners' backs. Frank searches for a missing Roy

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian returns to Darwin Ward, determined to make amends with Jac. But when forgiveness does not come easy, Kian goes into self-destruct mode - with almost fatal consequences. Meanwhile, a broken Essie searches for her purpose, but where will she find the answer she seeks? Ric suspects Guy has fallen off the wagon and sets out to stop him working at Holby, once and for all

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: With no money and Rory's health faltering, Agatha finds refuge in the storeroom of her old workplace. Meghan and Jack are at breaking point until Agatha takes a phone call from Hayden, who is in police custody. Laura Carmichael stars

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: In the final episode, the play's producer decides to draft in a voice of experience to get Michael and David in line. Meanwhile, Georgia shares David's work without him knowing, and he is pleasantly surprised by the enthusiastic response his work receives, and Michael seeks resolution on what has happened to his neighbour. Comedy, starring Michael Sheen and David Tennant

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Michelle Visage welcomes comedian Keith Lemon, who chats about the appeal of crafting in a pandemic. Former footballer Alex Scott rings to inspire Michelle with her ambitious workouts, and the presenter gets on a treadmill with bestie Leah Remini

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is proud of her working-class roots, but with the gap between rich and poor widening, she wants to know how easy it would be for her to move up a class. She overhauls her leisure activities with a spot of golf and some classical music, takes voice lessons to refine her accent and supercharges her table manners with an intense etiquette lesson. However, she soon discovers that there are obstacles in the way that can make social mobility very difficult

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: Award-winning documentary following the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The control room experiences a spike in calls for patients with breathing difficulties, and when Sherilee talks to a daughter calls whose mother has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, it proves tough for the call handler as her own mum had the same illness. Meanwhile, call handler Brogan has to guide a caller through lifesaving CPR while they wait for an ambulance, and there's a full emergency at John Lennon Airport when a plane loses contact with air traffic control

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: MasterChef's John Torode continues his alphabetical journey, with this episode's recipes linked by the letter S. Mary Berry prepares salmon with a fresh sorrel and spinach sauce, and Nigel Slater cooks sea salt chocolate snaps

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and garden designer Danny Clarke tackle a neglected and boring backyard in Birmingham for its owner Sophie, who wants the area full of birds, bees and butterflies

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Matt Baker visit the Blean, an ancient woodland in the heart of Kent where they get an exclusive look at a unique wilding project. There is a report from Gloucestershire on the elusive pine marten, while one of the driest springs on record is causing big problems at Adam Henson's farm

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost brings viewers up-to-date on how his garden has been developing over the summer as well as offering advice on looking after and growing hydrangeas. In Devon, Toby Buckland shares tips on dealing with damaged shrubs, tree pruning and transplanting perennials, while Frances Tophill travels to West Sussex to meet a gardener and professor of biology whose garden has been planted with the conservation of insects in mind. Plus, a second chance to see Carol Klein's in-depth profile of a national collection of hydrangeas at Darley Park in Derby

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter demonstrates the difference between a bed of hardy annuals sown in a prepared bed alongside an unprepared bed, and George Anderson shows off his Joppa allotment

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's Spain
programme.desc: The chef continues his culinary tour in Castile - a region famous not only for its vast plains and castles but also as the home of 11th-century military leader El Cid - before heading to La Mancha, an area renowned for its rustic dishes and wine production, as well as being the land of Miguel de Cervantes' literary character Don Quixote

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Dish of the Day
programme.desc: The food writer turns the contents of his weekly shop into seven more meals, showing how items that he often buys can be made more exciting by concocting delicious new flavour combinations. The dishes he ends up with include ricotta beefburger, smoked mackerel coleslaw, and lemon and thyme cake

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: The penultimate leg of Si King and Dave Myers' European baking tour takes them to France. Travelling from Lyon to Carcassonne, they learn the secrets of teeth-pulling nougat, go truffle hunting and meet up with Dave's old flatmate, whom he has not seen for 30 years. Culinary delights en route include tarte aux pralines, a savoury-stuffed brioche, apricot tarte Tatin and, of course, the traditional French baguette

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley and broadcaster Ashley John-Baptiste reveal the inspiring stories of people across the UK who lead spiritually and emotionally fulfilling lives. In this first edition, they set out to meet the people who have found inner peace, calm and equilibrium among the hustle and bustle of modern living. Kate takes to the open waters to experience how wild swimming has been a life-changer for some - and even a life-saver for others. Meanwhile, Ashley discovers how a little time living as a hermit could ease the pressures of busy lives

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From the RAF Museum in Cosford, Shropshire, where antique experts Jonathan Pratt and Adam Partridge find a First World War pigeon capsule and a large collection of toy cars

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: The fourth and final part of a look back at the Games, focusing on the second half of the Games, and featuring cyclist Chris Hoy claim a second gold medal in the keirin

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: An exploration of the upland area of North Yorkshire, where heather-clad uplands and sheltered dales have been shaped both by the elements and by its people. Hardy Swaledale sheep roam the high moors all year round only coming down to the lowlands for lambing and shearing. Red grouse feed and breed amongst the heather alongside curlews and other ground nesting birds and billions of purple heather blooms provide nectar for the bees, brought to the moors by beekeepers in the summer. Toby Jones narrates, as time-lapse camera techniques bring fresh insight into the restless rhythms of the region

programme.title: Test Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from day four of the Second Test in the three-game series, held at Old Trafford. It is the first time this venue has hosted a Test match since last September, when England lost the Fourth Test of the 2019 Ashes series to Australia. With analysis from Michael Vaughan and commentary by Alastair Cook, Carlos Brathwaite, Phil Tufnell and Alison Mitchell

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani assess the profit-making potential of an eco-friendly entrepreneur's alternative to straws, a range of skincare products, a device designed to make bikes more visible, and a brand of food with a personal story behind it. Evan Davis presents

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan: From My Sofa
programme.desc: Romesh catches up with old friends from Albania, Mongolia and The Arctic, revisiting some of the highlights and introducing never-before-seen moments such as his near-death experience in a high-speed Mongolia car rally

programme.title: Trump in Tweets
programme.desc: Documentary taking an in-depth look at US President Donald Trump's use of social media, examining the impact of his tweets on politics both in America and across the world. With access to people in his inner circle, interviews with experts and journalists, as well as some of his most avid Twitter followers, the programme tells the story of how Trump has used Twitter as a tool to shape policy, conduct diplomacy and wage war on the establishment. Narrated by The Vivienne

programme.title: Donald Trump: All-American Billionaire
programme.desc: In a programme first shown in 2010, well before he became US President, Emily Maitlis profiles the property tycoon, exploring the controversy surrounding his plans to develop a golf resort on sand dunes near Aberdeen. She also investigates how he amassed his multi-billion-dollar fortune, and discovers why his extravagant lifestyle serves to reinforce his business brand. Featuring interviews with Trump himself, as well as members of his family and TV producer Mark Burnett, who helped make the businessman one of America's biggest small-screen stars on the original version of The Apprentice

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A report from Salford as the city lifts the lockdown and tries to get its community back to work. The programme follows the mayor and his team, looking at the burden that has fallen on local councils, and also showing the work done by teachers, bus drivers and those helping the homeless. But for Salford it has been expensive. With the threat of bankruptcy looming, can the council provide the support and services the locals need to get them through the next phase of the health and economic crisis?

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: Advanced paramedic Kirsten is faced with an unusual job. A call has come in from police about a burglar who has injured himself trying to escape from the house he was looting. As she arrives, she finds him in agony with a nasty open fracture on his leg. Later, Kelsey and Dave are called to a pregnant woman in a lot of pain who worries she may be in early labour, and Gareth and Gabby treat a woman who is struggling with her breathing. But then two high-priority calls come in - both of which involve children

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Nicky wakes up in hospital to find her overbearing mother interfering in both her work and personal life. She takes the opportunity to reassess her priorities. Fletch and Essie befriend a cancer patient, but the relationship forces all three of them to confront some ugly truths about what the future holds. Guy tries to prove he still has what it takes, and tries to persuade Max to approve a risky operation

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three talented artists to make something special for the homes of people they've never met. In Lincolnshire, a buyer is looking for a gift for her husband as a thank-you for all he does for the family and to mark his long service to charity work. Will a stone sculptor, a mosaic maker or an illustrator create the perfect present? Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades explores the First World War's legacy on the UK today, through archive and delving deeper into three barn fixes - some bagpipes, a soldier's wire cutters and two RAF mascots

programme.title: Blue Planet Revisited
programme.desc: Scientists find out what the future holds for the Great Barrier Reef, monitoring green turtles, following families of breeding birds and meeting the reef's curious residents

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock.

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Examining the heartland of the country, home to ancient structures such as the Great Wall and modern buildings like Beijing's Olympic Stadium, as well as animals including the giant panda and golden snub-nosed monkey. However, the region holds even deeper cultural significance as the land of origin of China's most populous ethnic group the Han people - whose 5,000-year-old traditions are increasingly under threat

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces dishes that are good to make in advance, including Mary Berry's fresh fruit salad, James Martin's chicken stew, the Hairy Bikers' autumn pudding, Nigel Slater's bean soup, and Nigella Lawson's ice-cream cake

programme.title: Wild Brazil
programme.desc: Stephen Mangan narrates a documentary following capuchin monkeys, giant otters and coatis as they strive to raise their families. The first episode sees the young animals taking their first steps in some of the world's most extraordinary habitats, where they have to beware of deadly predators including the jaguar and caiman

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: The first of five programmes looking back at the 2016 Rio Olympics, focusing on the opening six days - which included Adam Peaty's 100m breaststroke gold medal in the pool

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Catherine Southon and James Lewis scour a crowd at Ushaw in Co Durham to find objects to take off to auction. Catherine comes across a toy speedboat in its original box and James discovers a rare poster that came from a morgue which shows the average weight of adult organs. Host Paul Martin looks at some rare Catholic artefacts that survived the English Reformation

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Part two of two. Michael Portillo concludes his journey from Adelaide to Perth, striking gold and playing cards in Kalgoorlie, and uncovering a dark chapter of the past in Perth

programme.title: Test Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from day five of the Second Test in the three-game series, held at Old Trafford. England are hoping to avenge a series defeat away to the Windies last summer. With analysis from Michael Vaughan and commentary by Alastair Cook, Carlos Brathwaite, Phil Tufnell and Alison Mitchell

programme.title: Our Friend Victoria
programme.desc: The comedy of Victoria Wood is celebrated by Richard E Grant, with characters including Kimberley's friend, Jim Broadbent's northern playwright and Patricia Routledge's extraordinary monologues as Kitty. With contributions from Julie Walters and Michael Ball, as well as rare behind the scenes photos from her personal archive

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the second of the student quiz's first-round matches, four students from the University of Leicester pit their wits against a team from Durham University in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: A look at the impact of the insurgency from the perspectives of American soldier Lt Col Nate Sassaman and Baghdadi Alaa Adel, who was 12 when she suffered life-changing injuries. Unprepared for the hostile environment he found himself in, Sassaman was pushed to the very darkest regions of his psyche. Alaa was hit in the face by shrapnel from one of the first roadside bombs planted by insurgents and intended for American forces. Looking back at that time, both question the benefits of the war in Iraq

programme.title: Strasbourg 1518
programme.desc: Jonathan Glazer's short dance film inspired by an event in which an involuntary mania took hold of citizens in the city of Strasbourg just over 500 years ago

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Topical comedy quiz show, hosted by Dara O Briain. Regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of with the help of guests Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, Rhys James, Nish Kumar and Tiff Stevenson. Stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Million Dollar Wedding Planner
programme.desc: The exclusive world of Lelian Chew, who organises nuptials for the super-rich in the Far East, taking on 10 just ceremonies a year at an average cost of $1million each. Bespoke Cartier jewellery, hundreds of thousands of dollars of flowers and multiple designer wedding dresses are nothing out of the ordinary. But Lelian's job also involves navigating the clash between age-old Chinese marriage customs and the desires of the young, often with outlandish solutions - multiple weddings are becoming the norm for the Chinese super-rich

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Matt Baker visit the Blean, an ancient woodland in the heart of Kent where they get an exclusive look at a unique wilding project. There is a report from Gloucestershire on the elusive pine marten, while one of the driest springs on record is causing big problems at Adam Henson's farm

programme.title: Saving Our Nurses
programme.desc: Newly qualified physiotherapist Esther dreads her forthcoming move from the neurology department to outpatients and the proposed switch is causing her some worrying health problems. Occupational therapist Melissa cares deeply for her patients, but her dyslexia and the demands of essential paperwork means she's losing motivation for her job

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Actor Phil Daniels, drag artist Baga Chipz, Olympic hockey gold medallist and sports presenter Sam Quek, conductor for the Kingdom Choir Karen Gibson and TV presenter Dominic Littlewood compete in the kitchen. For their first challenge, they head to the MasterChef Market - stocked full of the best quality produce from across the world including meat, fish, fruit, vegetables, nuts, pulses, herbs and spices. The challenge is to invent and then cook one dish to show the judges they have potential. Presented by John Torode and Gregg Wallace

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A fibre artist, a ceramicist and an abstract painter create artworks for a Staffordshire who woman wants a piece to mark the birth of her first grandchild. Nick Knowles presents

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: With the help of archive, Jay Blades charts how three inventions fixed in the barn - a magic lantern, projector and mutoscope - helped influence modern TV and film technology

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: From her garden at Glebe Cottage in Devon, Carol Klein goes back to the basics of propagating plants by demonstrating how to take cuttings. There is also an interview with Advolly Richmond, whose love of garden history is reflected in her own garden, and she shares her passion for a flower with a surprising past. Joe Swift has a large plant in his London garden that is due for some timely remedial work, and the team travels to Brighton to visit a garden packed with exotic plants grown in raised beds and containers

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The series concludes with a visit to the country's eastern seaboard to investigate how the Chinese government is managing environmental concerns while meeting the ever-expanding needs of the population. Among the region's diverse locales lie futuristic cities, seaweed-thatched villages and rugged wetlands that are home to some of the world's rarest animal species

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Nick Crane explores the wealth of wildlife attracted to the Firth of Forth. Tessa Dunlop reveals how the Victorian zeal for cleanliness turned the Thames into a giant toilet bowl

programme.title: Wild Brazil
programme.desc: The animal families face the formidable monsoon rains and the giant otter cubs must watch out for predatory jaguars and learn to swim before their river floods. The coatis have to eat as much as they can ahead of their habitat disappearing underwater, while the youngest capuchin monkey needs to master some clever survival skills. Stephen Mangan narrates

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: A look back at days seven and eight of the Rio Olympics, where British focus was on the likes of Mo Farah, Bradley Wiggins, Helen Glover and Laura Kenny

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Ushaw in Co Durham, antique experts David Fletcher and James Lewis put a value on the region's antiques and collectibles. David is delighted by a 1950s toy plane, while James finds a 1940s menu from a famous Hollywood restaurant which has been signed by some top film stars. Host Paul Martin visits the Mining Art Gallery in Bishop Auckland to find out why many coal miners were inspired to pick up a paintbrush

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Jeremy Vine in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the south east of Australia from Canberra, the seat of government, to the multi-ethnic heart of its second largest city, Melbourne. Floating above Canberra in a hot-air balloon, Michael sees how Australia's 'Bush Capital' was planned in the early 20th century and tours the nation's Parliament. He takes the high-speed train nearly 300 miles south west, to arrive in Melbourne at the head of Port Philip Bay on Australia's south-eastern coast

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Ellises sample life in the 1970s, an age of power cuts and strikes, but also a golden era for working class families. They enjoy rare time together helped with the advent of their first record player and car, while actress Claire Sweeney brings 'Scouse', her family's speciality, round for tea. Sara Cox and Polly Russell present

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti join the staff working in some of the most unusual hotels around the world, learning the tricks of the trade along the way. This time, Giles and Monica help out at The Torridon in the Scottish Highlands, Britain's most northerly five-star hotel. Its need to balance a luxury stay with being self-sufficient has Monica collecting seaweed for the kitchen and helping to herd Highland cattle, while Giles learns to change toilet seats and joins the staff in their afternoon off - for a swim in the beautiful but freezing-cold loch

programme.title: The Rise of the Murdoch Dynasty
programme.desc: A huge scandal threatens Rupert's dream of passing on the family business to one of his children, resulting in 'the most humble day' of his life. In response to the revelations, the Murdoch Empire goes on the offensive and the alliance against him come under sustained pressure, but whistle-blowers begin to emerge, then a bombshell revelation completely changes public opinion. A sequence of disastrous events leaves Murdoch's business plans in ruins, and he closes the News of the World newspaper

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Jon Richardson, team captain on 8 Out of 10 Cats, hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, west London, warming up the crowd before introducing routines by fellow comedians Sara Pascoe and Nathan Caton

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan: From My Sofa
programme.desc: Romesh catches up with old friends from Albania, Mongolia and The Arctic, revisiting some of the highlights and introducing never-before-seen moments such as his near-death experience in a high-speed Mongolia car rally

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades narrates a spin-off show delving deeper into three repairs to show how the heirlooms help shaped modern life, beginning with the story of toys

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George Anderson keeps an eye on his cuttings and Kirsty Wilson is extra careful as she shows how to propagate cacti. There is good news and bad news on an allotment in Aberdeen

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants are split into pairs and tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour - but there's a catch. They are separated by a wall, meaning the key to the task is communication as one pair recreates John Torode's pan-fried cod wrapped in ham and the other tackles his calf's liver and champ mash. For the second task, the competitors are sent out on their first mass catering challenge, at the McLaren Technology Centre in Woking, Surrey

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: A BBC Grand Virtual Orchestra made up of more than 300 BBC musicians perform Beethoveniana by composer Iain Farrington, a brand new reworking of Beethoven's nine symphonies commissioned by the BBC Proms to celebrate the opening of the 2020 Proms season

programme.title: BBC Proms Classics
programme.desc: Katie Derham presents this 2017 concert by the City of Birmingham Symphony Orchestra, conducted by Mirga Grazinyte-Tyla. The show features Beethoven's Fifth Symphony, Stravinsky's Violin Concerto with soloist Leila Josefowicz, and a piece by Gerald Barry, featuring tenor Allan Clayton

programme.title: The Merchant of Venice: Royal Shakespeare Company
programme.desc: When a contract between Bassanio and Shylock is broken in Venice, simmering racial tensions boil over. A wronged father, and despised outsider, Shylock looks to exact the ultimate price for a deal sealed in blood. Polly Findlay directs this RSC production of Shakespeare's uncompromising play, with Makram J Khoury, Patsy Ferran and Jamie Ballard

programme.title: Institute
programme.desc: In a shadowy establishment, residents attempt to create order from the chaos of life. Carers become patients, memories fracture and relationships collide. Drama by the physical theatre company Gecko, starring Chris Evans and Vanessa Guevara Flores

programme.title: Shakespeare in Italy
programme.desc: The historian concludes his tour of the country beginning with a visit to Venice, where he meets actor Ciaran Hinds to discuss why the Bard chose the city as the setting for The Merchant of Venice. Francesco is then joined by Mark Rylance in Rome as they explore how the playwright used the Italian capital for the plots of Julius Caesar and Antony and Cleopatra to avoid Elizabethan censors. Finally, on the island of Stromboli, off the northern coast of Sicily, Francesco uncovers the location for Shakespeare's late masterpiece - The Tempest

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe marks the final round of the FA Cup by providing football fans with a chance to revisit some of the best (and worst) songs produced to celebrate the tournament's conclusion over the years. Viewers at home will be able to make use of social media to vote for their favourite tunes, meaning that woeful ditties such as Manchester United's 1996 song Move Move Move, featuring a youthful Paul Scholes, David Beckham and the Neville brothers, could feature alongside the arguably more 'professional' sounding efforts of the London Community Gospel Choir, which sang Abide With Me - the FA Cup's traditional anthem - ahead of Chelsea's clash with Everton at Wembley in 2009

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Roxy struggles with her betrayal of Ronnie, Christian reaches breaking point with Steven, while Sean careers dangerously out of control

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Stacey's search for Sean takes her to Tanya's doorstep, Ian sets out to bring Lucy home, and Steven makes a serious accusation

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Roxy turns to Christian for support, while Bianca strikes up a feud with the Masoods. Gus bids farewell to the Square, but not before gaining revenge on Sean

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian's investigations lead to the truth about Steven, and Bianca tries Ricky's patience by refusing to back down to Zainab's demands

programme.title: Coastal Path
programme.desc: Paul Rose sets off on the walk of a lifetime - 630 miles of the South West Coast Path. The explorer hopes to discover wildlife, wild traditions and wild adventure at every turn

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a wintry scene in which the glistening sun peeks through the trees to warm the cold day

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Antiques Roadshow's Paul Atterbury recalls the heyday of the great ocean liners, travelling round Britain to reveal a story of design, politics and tragedy as he finds out how these great ships captured the public's imagination. Along the way he uncovers a cinema in Scotland built from the interiors of the SS Homeric, and visits a house in Poole, Dorset, where cabins from the Mauretania have been preserved

programme.title: Being Beethoven
programme.desc: As the composer embarked on the extraordinary flowering of his late music, his health deteriorated and his desire for love and family tipped over into obsession. Musical highlights include Paul Lewis exploring the beauty and brutality of the Diabelli Variations, and the Takacs Quartet playing the Hymn of Thanksgiving from Op 132

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures into the Black Forest to discover the essence of Germany, and at Goettingen he discovers two sides of student life at the turn of the 20th century

programme.title: Games Britannia
programme.desc: Historian and broadcaster Benjamin Woolley explores the history of games in Britain. He discovers playing games was associated with gambling in the late Middle Ages, but a moral backlash in Victorian times transformed them into tools for moral education - and led to the development of the world's first games industry, with ludo, snakes & ladders and the Staunton chess set among the biggest sellers

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary about the history of darts, as it transformed from a traditional working-class pub game into a world-recognised sport, and the role television played in elevating competitors such as Alan Evans and Jocky Wilson into household names. With contributions from Eric Bristow, Bobby George, John Lowe and Phil Taylor. Narrated by Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Gauguin - A Dangerous Life
programme.desc: A biography of Paul Gauguin, a committed multi-media artist who worked with the Impressionists and had a tempestuous relationship with Vincent van Gogh. He left his wife to bring up five children and used his colonial privilege to travel to Polynesia, where in his 40s he took 'wives' aged 13-15, creating images of them and their world that promoted a fantasy paradise of an unspoilt Eden in the Pacific

programme.title: A History of Art in Three Colours
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox turns his attention to blue, examining how the colour was originally created from lapis lazuli, an imported precious stone found in the East, and that the bright paint it produced captivated artists in the Middle Ages. He explores the renowned Florentine painter Giotto's ground-breaking religious frescoes in the Arena Chapel in Padua, and charts how the colour has also helped painters from more recent times, including Gaugin, Van Gogh and Picasso, to depict emotional depth

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a wintry scene in which the glistening sun peeks through the trees to warm the cold day

programme.title: Being Beethoven
programme.desc: As the composer embarked on the extraordinary flowering of his late music, his health deteriorated and his desire for love and family tipped over into obsession. Musical highlights include Paul Lewis exploring the beauty and brutality of the Diabelli Variations, and the Takacs Quartet playing the Hymn of Thanksgiving from Op 132

programme.title: Coastal Path
programme.desc: Traversing the rugged and mysterious north Cornish coast, explorer Paul Rose's first stop is Morwenstow, where he squeezes into the Trust's smallest property, the Hawker's Hut. Built in the 1800s by opium-smoking poet Reverend Hawker, it is popular with walkers who add their own graffiti. Next, Paul enjoys a tour of the wild and majestic Tintagel Castle, before heading to Padstow where the Mayday 'Obby 'Oss celebrations are in full swing. Paul is then put through his paces by a group of youngsters training to be surf lifesavers at Newquay's Fistral beach

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: As night settles over a rocky coast, Bob Ross produces a warm glow in the sky in a painting on black canvas

programme.title: Bob Ross: The Happy Painter
programme.desc: Documentary taking a behind-the-scenes look at American painter Bob Ross's journey from humble beginnings to pop culture icon, charting how one local TV commercial evolved into a viral phenomenon that continues to inspire millions around the world. The film reveals the public and private sides of Bob Ross through accounts from close friends and family, childhood photographs and rare archive footage. Interviewees recount his gentle, mild-mannered demeanour and unwavering dedication to wildlife as well as disclosing little-known facts about his famous curly hair - which was naturally straight - and his fascination with fast cars

programme.title: Empire of the Tsars: Romanov Russia with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley tells the story of the dynasty that ruled Russia for more than three centuries, including figures such as Peter the Great and Catherine the Great, the struggle against Napoleon in 1812, and the political murders of Nicholas II and his family in 1918. In the first edition, she investigates the beginning of the Romanovs' 300-year reign in Russia, and charts ruthless Tsar Peter the Great's rule, who acceded to the throne at the age of nine

programme.title: Russia on Four Wheels
programme.desc: Part one of two. Justin Rowlatt and Anita Rani set off on separate road trips across Russia from Sochi, the Black Sea resort hosting the 2014 Winter Olympics. Anita travels north and west in a Kombat, a Russian-built luxury armour-plated vehicle, and enters a thriving world of westernised entrepreneurs who are at the forefront of shaping a new and vibrant country. Meanwhile, Justin heads east in a Soviet-built UAZ jeep, encountering a world that seems hardly to have changed since the Cold War

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of the world's most outstanding rhythmic gymnast Margarita Mamun, the leading representative of the Russian national team. The film presents a groundbreaking year in her life, from the 2015 World Championships to the 2016 Olympic Games, offering unprecedented access to the hidden world of elite gymnasts and the challenging training regime demanded by the Russian system

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank: At Home with the British
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank explores Britain's love affair with the terrace, charting the highs of Victorian industry, the lows of their post-war decline and on to their 21st-century regeneration - which for one street in Toxteth, Liverpool, even saw them awarded the 2015 Turner Prize

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Multimedia artist Rachel Maclean's film exploring societal pressures on women from the media. Two inmates in a surreal prison are forced to compete against each other in performing a series of strange tasks, but they find a way to subvert the rules of the system and expose the sinister truth that underpins their world

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manchester United v Chelsea (Kick-off 6.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the last-four match at Wembley Stadium. The sides last met here in the final of this competition two years ago, when Chelsea prevailed 1-0 courtesy of an Eden Hazard penalty. However, United are unbeaten in the six meetings between the clubs in all competitions and have defeated the Blues three times this season, completing a double over them in the league and winning at Stamford Bridge in the fourth round of the Carabao Cup

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the historic Buckfast Abbey in Devon, where experts examine an 18th-century tankard, an iron chest with a hidden lock dating back to the 1640s, London street signs, a Biba frock, and a piece of shrapnel from the battleship Bismarck

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Anna is tried for murder, but Emery is still missing - along with her alibi - and Moody refuses to act on her behalf as he believes the case to be indefensible. However, due to a mix-up the lawyer ends up with Lauderback's trunk and discovers a series of letters from Crosbie that reveal a startling fact. Lydia hosts a seance to make contact with Emery, intending to implicate Anna further, but instead she channels the spirit of Sook and inadvertently accuses Carver of murder. Period drama, starring Eva Green

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The World Health Organisation urged countries to stamp out infections as soon as they developed, but the UK government's initial scientific advice said the route out of the crisis was for most of us to catch the virus so we could develop 'herd immunity'. Dr Faye Kirkland investigates this approach to the Covid-19 pandemic, asking whether it was a dangerous gamble with people's lives or a sound scientific method. Faced with a growing backlash and warnings that the NHS was close to being overwhelmed, politicians denied herd immunity was the policy and within a few days Britain pivoted to a new strategy - lockdown

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Diane Parish and Tameka Empson, aka sisters Denise and Kim Fox, for a socially-distanced reunion as Tameka returns to set for the first time since 2019. The pair reveal all about their close working relationship and tell the stories behind some of their biggest moments - from Tameka reliving her infamous live labour scenes for the show's 30th anniversary to Diane explaining why she once had to pretend to all her friends and family that she'd quit the soap

programme.title: The Story of the Royal Welsh Show
programme.desc: With this year's event cancelled due to the pandemic, Gareth Wyn Jones looks at the history of the agricultural event, from its establishment in 1904 through to its status today as the pinnacle of the Welsh rural calendar

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: Agatha and Hayden attend an emotional vigil for missing baby Ben, while Megan and Jack's relationship is deteriorating under the strain. Agatha begins to panic when she realises that 'Rory' is becoming seriously unwell, but she is unwilling to seek medical help.

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: Simon brings in a friend to try to convince his two stars to put their faith in him, while Michael worries about a neighbour when he spots an ambulance outside next door. David not only manages to keep the house in order and the home-schooling ticking over in Georgia's absence, but also gets around to finishing a draft of his screenplay

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: The two business partners head out on a double date, with Kazim the one in love and Jeremiah his reluctant wingman. Out of their comfort zone, they head to the East End to meet the girls, but what follows is a very strange dinner - which goes from bad to worse very quickly. Return of the comedy, starring Kayode Ewumi and Trieve Blackwood-Cambridge

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Deborah Cox hosts as the contestants do battle in a song-and-dance diss-track showdown, with judges Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Filly meets his match with eccentric Adam, a louder-than-life student who loves to sing about gonorrhoea

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, Mo Gilligan and Anne-Marie pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, and Jamie Redknapp takes on Dina Asher-Smith in The Sporting Fix. Plus, Zara Larsson performs in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: In an episode originally broadcast on 27 October 1998, Pat and Peggy clash spectacularly following an accusation that Frank and Pat are having an affair behind their partners' backs. Frank searches for a missing Roy

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian returns to Darwin Ward, determined to make amends with Jac. But when forgiveness does not come easy, Kian goes into self-destruct mode - with almost fatal consequences. Meanwhile, a broken Essie searches for her purpose, but where will she find the answer she seeks? Ric suspects Guy has fallen off the wagon and sets out to stop him working at Holby, once and for all

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: With no money and Rory's health faltering, Agatha finds refuge in the storeroom of her old workplace. Meghan and Jack are at breaking point until Agatha takes a phone call from Hayden, who is in police custody. Laura Carmichael stars

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: In the final episode, the play's producer decides to draft in a voice of experience to get Michael and David in line. Meanwhile, Georgia shares David's work without him knowing, and he is pleasantly surprised by the enthusiastic response his work receives, and Michael seeks resolution on what has happened to his neighbour. Comedy, starring Michael Sheen and David Tennant

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is proud of her working-class roots, but with the gap between rich and poor widening, she wants to know how easy it would be for her to move up a class. She overhauls her leisure activities with a spot of golf and some classical music, takes voice lessons to refine her accent and supercharges her table manners with an intense etiquette lesson. However, she soon discovers that there are obstacles in the way that can make social mobility very difficult

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Michelle Visage welcomes comedian Keith Lemon, who chats about the appeal of crafting in a pandemic. Former footballer Alex Scott rings to inspire Michelle with her ambitious workouts, and the presenter gets on a treadmill with bestie Leah Remini

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: Award-winning documentary following the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The control room experiences a spike in calls for patients with breathing difficulties, and when Sherilee talks to a daughter calls whose mother has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, it proves tough for the call handler as her own mum had the same illness. Meanwhile, call handler Brogan has to guide a caller through lifesaving CPR while they wait for an ambulance, and there's a full emergency at John Lennon Airport when a plane loses contact with air traffic control

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather
