programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manchester United v Chelsea (Kick-off 6.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the last-four match at Wembley Stadium. The sides last met here in the final of this competition two years ago, when Chelsea prevailed 1-0 courtesy of an Eden Hazard penalty. However, United are unbeaten in the six meetings between the clubs in all competitions and have defeated the Blues three times this season, completing a double over them in the league and winning at Stamford Bridge in the fourth round of the Carabao Cup

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the historic Buckfast Abbey in Devon, where experts examine an 18th-century tankard, an iron chest with a hidden lock dating back to the 1640s, London street signs, a Biba frock, and a piece of shrapnel from the battleship Bismarck

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Anna is tried for murder, but Emery is still missing - along with her alibi - and Moody refuses to act on her behalf as he believes the case to be indefensible. However, due to a mix-up the lawyer ends up with Lauderback's trunk and discovers a series of letters from Crosbie that reveal a startling fact. Lydia hosts a seance to make contact with Emery, intending to implicate Anna further, but instead she channels the spirit of Sook and inadvertently accuses Carver of murder. Period drama, starring Eva Green

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Bournemouth v Southampton at the Vitality Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Leicester City at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: A truce with the Weekday Warriors proves short-lived, Alaska sets Miles up on his very first date at the school's basketball game, and Takumi has a suspicion who the rat might be

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: Miles agrees to spend Thanksgiving with Alaska, but a trip to the Colonel's home for the holiday that was designed to bring everyone together only leaves the group further apart

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: The return of the programme offering advice about which foods are healthy or harmful. Chris Bavin investigates worrying reports that eating chicken and turkey could cause cancer, and Gloria Hunniford looks at the trend for feeding pets raw meat

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development of a semi in Mottingham in south-east London, a detached in Littleover, Derby and a terrace in Dover in Kent

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles introduces footage of near misses caught on CCTV, including a couple out walking along the pavement who were hit by an out-of-control car

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Steph Connell and Thomas Forrester help teams look for items at Nottingham racecourse, while presenter Anita Manning gets to the bottom of a local UFO sighting

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Death reunites the residents once again, but some relationships are beyond repair, while Sabine pulls an outrageous stunt. Australian drama, starring Bridie McKim

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps an Anglo-Icelandic couple, who need to relocate from Reykjavik to Aberdeenshire on their £380,000 budget before their daughter starts university in the region

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A fibre artist, a ceramicist and an abstract painter create artworks for a Staffordshire who woman wants a piece to mark the birth of her first grandchild. Nick Knowles presents

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: With the help of archive, Jay Blades charts how three inventions fixed in the barn - a magic lantern, projector and mutoscope - helped influence modern TV and film technology

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: The horticultural designer encourages people across Ireland to get out in their gardens, taking calls, answering questions and offering a range of practical tips. In the first edition, he helps design a garden for Mary Fitzpatrick in Co Wicklow, and creates a sunken fire pit and seating area in his own patch

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Diane Parish and Tameka Empson, aka sisters Denise and Kim Fox, for a socially-distanced reunion as Tameka returns to set for the first time since 2019. The pair reveal all about their close working relationship and tell the stories behind some of their biggest moments - from Tameka reliving her infamous live labour scenes for the show's 30th anniversary to Diane explaining why she once had to pretend to all her friends and family that she'd quit the soap

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The World Health Organisation urged countries to stamp out infections as soon as they developed, but the UK government's initial scientific advice said the route out of the crisis was for most of us to catch the virus so we could develop 'herd immunity'. Dr Faye Kirkland investigates this approach to the Covid-19 pandemic, asking whether it was a dangerous gamble with people's lives or a sound scientific method. Faced with a growing backlash and warnings that the NHS was close to being overwhelmed, politicians denied herd immunity was the policy and within a few days Britain pivoted to a new strategy - lockdown

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: Agatha and Hayden attend an emotional vigil for missing baby Ben, while Megan and Jack's relationship is deteriorating under the strain. Agatha begins to panic when she realises that 'Rory' is becoming seriously unwell, but she is unwilling to seek medical help.

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: Simon brings in a friend to try to convince his two stars to put their faith in him, while Michael worries about a neighbour when he spots an ambulance outside next door. David not only manages to keep the house in order and the home-schooling ticking over in Georgia's absence, but also gets around to finishing a draft of his screenplay

programme.title: Northern Ireland on The One Show
programme.desc: Jennie Browne and Joe Lindsay present reports from the magazine programme that were made in Northern Ireland, includes a woman's attempt to break a nearly 90-year-old record for swimming from Donegal to Portstewart

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: The two business partners head out on a double date, with Kazim the one in love and Jeremiah his reluctant wingman. Out of their comfort zone, they head to the East End to meet the girls, but what follows is a very strange dinner - which goes from bad to worse very quickly. Return of the comedy, starring Kayode Ewumi and Trieve Blackwood-Cambridge

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Deborah Cox hosts as the contestants do battle in a song-and-dance diss-track showdown, with judges Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, Mo Gilligan and Anne-Marie pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, and Jamie Redknapp takes on Dina Asher-Smith in The Sporting Fix. Plus, Zara Larsson performs in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Chris Bavin and Gloria Hunniford are joined by Edwina Currie as they examine conflicting media stories about the safety of eating eggs, 30 years on from the salmonella scandal

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large terraced house in Earlsfield in London, ancient woodland in Eythorne in Kent, and a building housing six flats in Tonypandy in Glamorgan are all sold at auction

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A Coastguard crew fights to save a film extra who is taken ill at the top of a cathedral tower, and customers at a pub run from an out-of-control lorry. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ochuko Ojiri and Jonathan Pratt help two teams scour an antiques fair at the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthenshire, while presenter Eric Knowles visits a rugby union club to see a collection of sporting footwear memorabilia

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Time together is revealing for Ana and Ryan, and also for Renee and Mark. Kam faces a tough interview

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin meets a couple of creative Californians keen to make a lifestyle leap to the Welsh borders, and also discovers how llamas are thriving in the British countryside

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges an impressionist painter, a metal sculptor and an artist who paints in a caravan to create artwork for a London couple who have just renovated their home

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades revisits three fixes from the barn - a polyphon, gramophone and transistor radio - to tell the story of how modern music has been influenced by vintage music players

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: In an episode originally broadcast on 27 October 1998, Pat and Peggy clash spectacularly following an accusation that Frank and Pat are having an affair behind their partners' backs. Frank searches for a missing Roy

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian returns to Darwin Ward, determined to make amends with Jac. But when forgiveness does not come easy, Kian goes into self-destruct mode - with almost fatal consequences. Meanwhile, a broken Essie searches for her purpose, but where will she find the answer she seeks? Ric suspects Guy has fallen off the wagon and sets out to stop him working at Holby, once and for all

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: With no money and Rory's health faltering, Agatha finds refuge in the storeroom of her old workplace. Meghan and Jack are at breaking point until Agatha takes a phone call from Hayden, who is in police custody. Laura Carmichael stars

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: In the final episode, the play's producer decides to draft in a voice of experience to get Michael and David in line. Meanwhile, Georgia shares David's work without him knowing, and he is pleasantly surprised by the enthusiastic response his work receives, and Michael seeks resolution on what has happened to his neighbour. Comedy, starring Michael Sheen and David Tennant

programme.title: The Chronicles of Erne
programme.desc: The programme documents winter on the lough, with migratory birds from colder climates arriving and painter Lorna Smyth putting the finishing touches to her exhibition of landscapes. At Portora's lock gates, commercial fisherman Eugene Brazil and Roy Shaw clean and checking their nets for European silver eels

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Michelle Visage welcomes comedian Keith Lemon, who chats about the appeal of crafting in a pandemic. Former footballer Alex Scott rings to inspire Michelle with her ambitious workouts, and the presenter gets on a treadmill with bestie Leah Remini

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is proud of her working-class roots, but with the gap between rich and poor widening, she wants to know how easy it would be for her to move up a class. She overhauls her leisure activities with a spot of golf and some classical music, takes voice lessons to refine her accent and supercharges her table manners with an intense etiquette lesson. However, she soon discovers that there are obstacles in the way that can make social mobility very difficult

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: Award-winning documentary following the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The control room experiences a spike in calls for patients with breathing difficulties, and when Sherilee talks to a daughter calls whose mother has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, it proves tough for the call handler as her own mum had the same illness. Meanwhile, call handler Brogan has to guide a caller through lifesaving CPR while they wait for an ambulance, and there's a full emergency at John Lennon Airport when a plane loses contact with air traffic control

programme.title: Mayor's Question Time
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the London Assembly to Mayor Sadiq Khan from Thursday 16 July

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Women and Equalities select committee session on the impact of the coronavirus on BAME people with evidence from Kemi Badenoch, Minister at the Government Equalities Office, Jo Churchill MP, Minister for Prevention, Public Health and Primary Care and Chris Pincher, Minister for Housing, from Wednesday 15 July

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Prime Minister Boris Johnson

programme.title: Washington Journal
programme.desc: Highlights of the week from the USA

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The international outcry prompted by Beijing's imposition of a new national security law in Hong Kong has been long and loud - but will it make any difference? Inside the territory protests have been muted and the main pro-democracy activist movement has disbanded itself. HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to one of the co-founders of that movement, Nathan Law. He's now in self-imposed exile - is China's hardline Hong Kong strategy working?

programme.desc: Following the activities of a 10-week-old polar bear being raised by his mother in the Canadian Arctic. Percy was born during the winter in a den kept up to 40 degrees warmer than outside, where the temperature drops to minus 60C and it is dark for months. Polar bears have thick coats, a 10cm thick layer of blubber that acts like thermal underwear, and huge fur-covered paws that are like snowshoes

programme.title: All That Lies Behind Me (Sgeulachd Donald Merrett)
programme.desc: Using dramatic reconstructions and expert opinion, John Morrison revisits the story of John Donald Merrett - a murderer who, having been cleared of killing his mother in 1927, spent the next 30 years on a spree across Europe, spending a large inheritance on women and the black market. The programme also reveals how when Merrett returned to Britain, he killed his wife and mother-in-law, before committing suicide

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: The raccoons finally get the better of Mike while he tries to retrieve an object in their lair

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The bumbling blockhead is mistaken for an escaped convict and finds himself behind bars - where he soon makes himself at home while the real baddie has an awful time in the hands of Bean's landlady

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having learned Master Wu is the 'Treacherous One,' the ninja find themselves under siege at the Monastery, trying to protect Wu from the wrath of Aspheera.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Ben and Gwen can't wait for the Omni-Copter to arrive

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp present the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games. They are joined by Roman's I'm a Celebrity buddy Myles Stephenson, singer-songwriter Anne-Marie and operatic star Katherine Jenkins

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: The chef is joined by Brian Turner to explore the culinary delights of his native Yorkshire, with James visiting Frances Atkins' restaurant The Yorke Arms, where he enjoys her signature dish al fresco in her unusual dining room the Onion. Brian tastes the delights of some local charcuterie and is challenged to some off-road driving, while James brushes up his clay-pigeon shooting skills

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare pork belly served with Lisa's potato salad and a salsa verde, vegetarian stuffed squash, and chocolate and coconut pie

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: The baking expert creates a spread for a winter themed party with the help of DJ Dev Griffin, who learns how to make Juliet's winter woodland mushroom cake. Other menu highlights are meringue lollies, s'mores cupcakes, pigs in blankets and filo pastry pies

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis presents the junior version of the singing talent contest, giving youngsters a chance to achieve stardom. The Voice UK's Will.i.am returns as one of the four coaches, with McFly's Danny Jones and fellow pop stars Pixie Lott and Paloma Faith occupying the other spinning red chairs. Youngsters between the ages of seven and 14 display their musical talents in a bid to win a £30,000 prize to contribute to their musical education, along with a family holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Black Widow, Thingamajig, Butterfly, Leopard and Flamingo perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: In this special episode, Paul returns to Battersea Dogs & Cats Home to see how staff coped during one of the most extraordinary and dramatic periods in its history - the coronavirus pandemic. Before Battersea closes its doors to the public for the first time in 160 years, Paul spends one last day at the centre helping in whatever way he can, as the team goes all out against the clock to rehome more than 150 dogs and cats, puppies and kittens. Paul offers to foster a dog, but when he meets an adorable litter of puppies - and one crawls into his coat - can he hold his resolve, or will she become a permanent member of the O'Grady family?

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Jason Manford narrates a celebration of Weatherfield's most memorable weddings, of which there have been 130 weddings over the decades. Viewers will get to the chance to see some of the most dramatic, shocking and heart-warming occasions, including the big days of Raquel and Curly, Shelley and Charlie, and Beth and Kirk

programme.title: Diamonds Are Forever (1971)
programme.desc: James Bond investigates a diamond-smuggling operation, and discovers that behind the scheme is his insidious arch-enemy Blofeld, who is planning to construct a devastating orbital doomsday weapon powered by the precious stones, which will force world powers to disarm and give him global domination. Spy adventure, starring Sean Connery, Jill St John, Charles Gray, Bernard Lee and Lois Maxwell

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Margo takes great pride in her new job at the surgery, but after a chance encounter with a recluse, she begins to realise where her greatest talent lies. Louisa grows closer to Sven, but as Gerry befriends a convict and Leslie begins spending all his time in the local bars, she begins to suspect she has made a mistake bringing her children to Corfu

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Mary's past catches up with her when an unwelcome visitor delivers an ultimatum, as secrets and rifts threaten the unity of the family. News about the running of Downton Hospital puts Violet on the warpath, while those below stairs find social changes are putting their futures in jeopardy and Daisy's good intentions at the Mallerton House auction have disastrous consequences. Final series of the costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Michelle Dockery

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Plans for the wedding are gathering steam, but Lady Mary's meddling lands Carson in trouble with Mrs Hughes. Cora and Violet clash over plans for the hospital takeover, and Thomas's job search is proving disheartening. Mary comes up with a fresh idea that offers Anna new hope, prompting a trip to London, and Lady Edith faces challenges at the magazine although it's a problem closer to home that reaches breaking point. A day out for the family and servants ends in panic and leaves Robert facing a difficult decision

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: A look at comedy in 2003, when Tim, Lee and Dawn's love triangle reached a memorable conclusion in The Office and the original run of Cold Feet bowed out with a tragic storyline. It was also the year when members of the public found themselves the unwitting comedy stooges of hidden-camera shows like Trigger Happy TV and Three Non Blondes, Bruce Forsyth made a memorable appearance as guest host of Have I Got News for You and comedy legend Bob Monkhouse performed his last stand-up gig to an audience of fellow comics in a small London pub

programme.title: Last Laugh in Vegas
programme.desc: After adjusting to life in Nevada, and trying to impress flamboyant producer Frank Marino, Cannon and Ball, Anita Harris, Kenny Lynch, Bernie Clifton and Su Pollard take to the stage for their big gig in Las Vegas. With Jess Conrad, Mick Miller and Bobby Crush also hoping to wow the masses, it remains to be seen whether Frank's hard work has paid off

programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team head to Liverpool, where Henry Nicholls appraises an ice bucket, Michael Hogben expresses doubt about some photographic plates and Tracy Thackeray-Howitt assesses a selection of jewellery

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five NHS medical staff answer from Teesside questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lydia opens up to Chas, Harriet is left uneasy by Cain's threats, and Jimmy tells Nicola not to interfere

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff uses his hospital radio show to talk about how he has forgiven Yasmeen and wants to help her. Abi moves into No 13, and when Adam spots Sinead's cardigan poking out of his bag, Daniel is embarrassed

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey is nominated by Auntie Vera to take over her Pearly Queen title, but when Sharon finds out, she tries to outperform her sister to bag the role for herself. Meanwhile, Dorien reluctantly agrees to a family reunion. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Jason Manford narrates an edition that trawls the archives of the soap to look back at some of the most notorious baddies to ever walk the streets of Weatherfield, featuring the likes of Richard Hillman, Charlie Stubbs, Maya Sharma and Pat Phelan

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follow three stories of sons coming to terms with the past, including Marc Wolfe's struggle to form a bond with his birth mother Esther, who lives in New Zealand. However, his search for identity didn't end there as researchers were able to reunite him with his birth father David, and the pair have grown close. One significant hurdle remained in the form of introducing David to his adoptive father Terry, and cameras were there as Marc introduces his two dads to each other

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Broadchurch
programme.desc: Following recent events, Miller is more isolated than ever before as she tries to find out what happened at the clifftop hut. Meanwhile, the police are circling Susan as Nige grows increasingly desperate for her to leave Broadchurch. What is she hiding? And which members of the local community hold the key to Danny's death? Detective drama, starring David Tennant and Olivia Colman

programme.title: European Cup Winners: The British Pioneers
programme.desc: A look back at the early achievements of British clubs in Europe, including Celtic's European Cup triumph in 1967, and Manchester United's victory the following year, which came a decade after the Munich air disaster

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Action from the latest edition of the weekly AEW event, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: It is the annual Portwenn versus Port Carran gig race and school gig festival, and this time Penhale is the umpire and Morwenna is the Portwenn team captain. However, Morwenna is forced to find a replacement rower when Eric dislocates his finger. Meanwhile, Mel Hendy goes to see Martin at the surgery worried about a rash that could be infectious, but she ends up opening up to him about her concerns over her husband's drinking. Elsewhere, Martin and Louisa arrive home to find Chris Parsons waiting for them

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz, with six more contestants competing in Fastest Finger First to see who will sit in the hot seat and have the chance to take home the £1million jackpot. To make things easier, the players have four lifelines - Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50:50 and Ask the Host - at their disposal

programme.title: The Secret Mediterranean with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: The broadcaster begins the final leg of his trip in Egypt, peering down through the waves at Cleopatra's sunken palace in Alexandria, where the country's last queen romanced Julius Caesar and Mark Antony. On the divided island of Cyprus, he gains rare access to a buffer zone established in 1974 by the UN and discovers settlements frozen in time. His journey ends in Malta with a chance to visit mysterious underground caves

programme.title: The Sport Gives Back Awards
programme.desc: Jeanette Kwakye hosts the inaugural staging of the awards ceremony from the Royal Institution in London. The event celebrates and acknowledges the many charities, staff and volunteers that focus on improving the lives of others in the UK. The Sport Gives Back Awards is an evening dedicated to recognising those who have excelled in using sport and dance as a vehicle for social change

programme.desc: Mike wakes up in the bathtub…huh? The house has been turned upside down and the other animals are being weird. They're acting like zombies and carrying food out of the house.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: The raccoons finally get the better of Mike while he tries to retrieve an object in their lair

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The bumbling blockhead is mistaken for an escaped convict and finds himself behind bars - where he soon makes himself at home while the real baddie has an awful time in the hands of Bean's landlady

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having learned Master Wu is the 'Treacherous One,' the ninja find themselves under siege at the Monastery, trying to protect Wu from the wrath of Aspheera.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Ben and Gwen can't wait for the Omni-Copter to arrive

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp present the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games. They are joined by Roman's I'm a Celebrity buddy Myles Stephenson, singer-songwriter Anne-Marie and operatic star Katherine Jenkins

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: The chef is joined by Brian Turner to explore the culinary delights of his native Yorkshire, with James visiting Frances Atkins' restaurant The Yorke Arms, where he enjoys her signature dish al fresco in her unusual dining room the Onion. Brian tastes the delights of some local charcuterie and is challenged to some off-road driving, while James brushes up his clay-pigeon shooting skills

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare pork belly served with Lisa's potato salad and a salsa verde, vegetarian stuffed squash, and chocolate and coconut pie

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: The baking expert creates a spread for a winter themed party with the help of DJ Dev Griffin, who learns how to make Juliet's winter woodland mushroom cake. Other menu highlights are meringue lollies, s'mores cupcakes, pigs in blankets and filo pastry pies

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis presents the junior version of the singing talent contest, giving youngsters a chance to achieve stardom. The Voice UK's Will.i.am returns as one of the four coaches, with McFly's Danny Jones and fellow pop stars Pixie Lott and Paloma Faith occupying the other spinning red chairs. Youngsters between the ages of seven and 14 display their musical talents in a bid to win a £30,000 prize to contribute to their musical education, along with a family holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Black Widow, Thingamajig, Butterfly, Leopard and Flamingo perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: In this special episode, Paul returns to Battersea Dogs & Cats Home to see how staff coped during one of the most extraordinary and dramatic periods in its history - the coronavirus pandemic. Before Battersea closes its doors to the public for the first time in 160 years, Paul spends one last day at the centre helping in whatever way he can, as the team goes all out against the clock to rehome more than 150 dogs and cats, puppies and kittens. Paul offers to foster a dog, but when he meets an adorable litter of puppies - and one crawls into his coat - can he hold his resolve, or will she become a permanent member of the O'Grady family?

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Jason Manford narrates a celebration of Weatherfield's most memorable weddings, of which there have been 130 weddings over the decades. Viewers will get to the chance to see some of the most dramatic, shocking and heart-warming occasions, including the big days of Raquel and Curly, Shelley and Charlie, and Beth and Kirk

programme.title: Diamonds Are Forever (1971)
programme.desc: James Bond investigates a diamond-smuggling operation, and discovers that behind the scheme is his insidious arch-enemy Blofeld, who is planning to construct a devastating orbital doomsday weapon powered by the precious stones, which will force world powers to disarm and give him global domination. Spy adventure, starring Sean Connery, Jill St John, Charles Gray, Bernard Lee and Lois Maxwell

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Margo takes great pride in her new job at the surgery, but after a chance encounter with a recluse, she begins to realise where her greatest talent lies. Louisa grows closer to Sven, but as Gerry befriends a convict and Leslie begins spending all his time in the local bars, she begins to suspect she has made a mistake bringing her children to Corfu

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Mary's past catches up with her when an unwelcome visitor delivers an ultimatum, as secrets and rifts threaten the unity of the family. News about the running of Downton Hospital puts Violet on the warpath, while those below stairs find social changes are putting their futures in jeopardy and Daisy's good intentions at the Mallerton House auction have disastrous consequences. Final series of the costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Michelle Dockery

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Plans for the wedding are gathering steam, but Lady Mary's meddling lands Carson in trouble with Mrs Hughes. Cora and Violet clash over plans for the hospital takeover, and Thomas's job search is proving disheartening. Mary comes up with a fresh idea that offers Anna new hope, prompting a trip to London, and Lady Edith faces challenges at the magazine although it's a problem closer to home that reaches breaking point. A day out for the family and servants ends in panic and leaves Robert facing a difficult decision

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: A look at comedy in 2003, when Tim, Lee and Dawn's love triangle reached a memorable conclusion in The Office and the original run of Cold Feet bowed out with a tragic storyline. It was also the year when members of the public found themselves the unwitting comedy stooges of hidden-camera shows like Trigger Happy TV and Three Non Blondes, Bruce Forsyth made a memorable appearance as guest host of Have I Got News for You and comedy legend Bob Monkhouse performed his last stand-up gig to an audience of fellow comics in a small London pub

programme.title: Last Laugh in Vegas
programme.desc: After adjusting to life in Nevada, and trying to impress flamboyant producer Frank Marino, Cannon and Ball, Anita Harris, Kenny Lynch, Bernie Clifton and Su Pollard take to the stage for their big gig in Las Vegas. With Jess Conrad, Mick Miller and Bobby Crush also hoping to wow the masses, it remains to be seen whether Frank's hard work has paid off

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lydia opens up to Chas, Harriet is left uneasy by Cain's threats, and Jimmy tells Nicola not to interfere

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff uses his hospital radio show to talk about how he has forgiven Yasmeen and wants to help her. Abi moves into No 13, and when Adam spots Sinead's cardigan poking out of his bag, Daniel is embarrassed

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey is nominated by Auntie Vera to take over her Pearly Queen title, but when Sharon finds out, she tries to outperform her sister to bag the role for herself. Meanwhile, Dorien reluctantly agrees to a family reunion. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Jason Manford narrates an edition that trawls the archives of the soap to look back at some of the most notorious baddies to ever walk the streets of Weatherfield, featuring the likes of Richard Hillman, Charlie Stubbs, Maya Sharma and Pat Phelan

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follow three stories of sons coming to terms with the past, including Marc Wolfe's struggle to form a bond with his birth mother Esther, who lives in New Zealand. However, his search for identity didn't end there as researchers were able to reunite him with his birth father David, and the pair have grown close. One significant hurdle remained in the form of introducing David to his adoptive father Terry, and cameras were there as Marc introduces his two dads to each other

Meanwhile, the police are circling Susan as Nige grows increasingly desperate for her to leave Broadchurch. What is she hiding? And which members of the local community hold the key to Danny's death? Detective drama, starring David Tennant and Olivia Colman

programme.desc: The broadcaster begins the final leg of his trip in Egypt, peering down through the waves at Cleopatra's sunken palace in Alexandria, where the country's last queen romanced Julius Caesar and Mark Antony. On the divided island of Cyprus, he gains rare access to a buffer zone established in 1974 by the UN and discovers settlements frozen in time. His journey ends in Malta with a chance to visit mysterious underground caves

programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.desc: Carter has a heart attack and lapses into a deep coma, so Peter takes over the running of his company Pewterschmidt Industries, but becomes power-mad and ends up firing every member of the board of directors

programme.desc: Part one of two. The Griffins are stuck indoors during a blackout, so Peter passes the time by telling a story spoofing Star Wars. programme.desc: Stewie auditions in drag for his favourite TV show after discovering it is only searching for girls, and soon becomes a hit with viewers. Meg shocks the entire city by dating a conventionally attractive boy

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.desc: Meg starts a relationship with oversexed neighbour Quagmire after celebrating her 18th birthday, but Peter stops at nothing to end his daughter's fledgling romance

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.desc: Roger meets Meredith, the most interesting woman in Langley Falls, and he struggles to create a new, more fabulous persona to keep up with her. programme.desc: The Brown and Tubbs families try to salvage what is left of their holidays when a storm hits Stoolbend, while Cleveland Jr makes a surprising announcement about his religious beliefs

programme.title: The Perfect Man (2005)
programme.desc: Teenager Holly Hamilton grows tired of moving home every time her mother splits up with another boyfriend. When the family travels to New York, Holly is determined to stay in one place and vows to find her mum the ideal partner - even if she has to make him up. However, when the relationship starts to take off, Holly is faced with the impossible task of producing a perfect man to live up to her mother's inflated expectations. Romantic comedy, starring Hilary Duff, Heather Locklear and Chris Noth

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.desc: Carter has a heart attack and lapses into a deep coma, so Peter takes over the running of his company Pewterschmidt Industries, but becomes power-mad and ends up firing every member of the board of directors

programme.desc: Part one of two. The Griffins are stuck indoors during a blackout, so Peter passes the time by telling a story spoofing Star Wars. programme.desc: Comedy from Harry Hill, inspired by viewers' home videos, featuring a baby doing a spot of fracking, toddlers wrestling for a place in a good nursery, and the fun that can be had with a squashed Womble. Plus, another Video You'll Wish You'd Never Seen

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Exploding toilets, airborne chainsaws and a cat with a talent for playing the drums are among the video highlights submitted by fans for comedy appraisal by Harry Hill. Plus, a prankster reveals how Star Wars villain Darth Vader takes advantage of his unique powers during an embarrassing lavatory-based situation

programme.title: Roswell, New Mexico
programme.desc: Michael and Isobel learn the painful truth about the night Tripp attacked their mothers. Alex is desperate to know the truth about his troubled family history

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Chris go missing, prompting Lois to ask a psychic for help - but the family's obsession with the paranormal goes too far. Meanwhile, Chris develops a crush on a girl at school

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie auditions in drag for his favourite TV show after discovering it is only searching for girls, and soon becomes a hit with viewers. Meg shocks the entire city by dating a conventionally attractive boy

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter decides to become a member of the paparazzi, but falls out with his boss at the brewery over her inappropriate behaviour and ends up getting fired. Meanwhile, Stewie appears in a play but suffers stage fright

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg starts a relationship with oversexed neighbour Quagmire after celebrating her 18th birthday, but Peter stops at nothing to end his daughter's fledgling romance

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian is arrested for possession of cannabis, prompting him to begin a campaign for the drug to be legalised that succeeds beyond his wildest dreams, but results in the residents of Quahog becoming addicts

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger meets Meredith, the most interesting woman in Langley Falls, and he struggles to create a new, more fabulous persona to keep up with her. When he fails, Francine suggests the dejected alien embark on a journey of self-discovery, which doesn't go exactly as planned. Meanwhile, Stan and the others overdo it during a workout, and end up feeling too sore to move, giving Klaus the chance to throw himself a party

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan takes Francine to a baseball game to celebrate her birthday, but when the ground's 'kiss cam' broadcasts their particularly horrible kiss to the rest of the spectators, Stan begins to question everything about his marriage. Meanwhile, Steve and Roger head to Nevada to help clean up polluted water. Animated comedy, with the voice of Seth MacFarlane

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland Jr volunteers for a church trip after falling for a girl in the choir, but his friend Hunter criticises him for only attending to spend time with her. Meanwhile, Donna's ex-boyfriend returns from Iraq to discover she is now married. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of guest stars Darren Criss (Glee) and Fergie (Black Eyed Peas)

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: The Brown and Tubbs families try to salvage what is left of their holidays when a storm hits Stoolbend, while Cleveland Jr makes a surprising announcement about his religious beliefs

programme.title: The Almost Impossible Gameshow
programme.desc: Action-packed game show, with commentary from Irish comedy duo the Rubberbandits. Ten contestants are given 50 lives to take on a series of tricky challenges. The aim of the game is to complete five of them without losing all of their lives. Challenges include Tiny Bike, Dizzy Hurdle and Groin Croissant

programme.title: Release the Hounds
programme.desc: Horror game show, hosted by Reggie Yates, in which two brothers and a sister enter a forest at dusk and endure a series of spooky challenges during which they must unlock chests full of prize money. In order to win the jackpot they need to get back out again, but the cash is protected by a pack of angry dogs

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: An evening of watching telly is ruined when the set breaks down _ but worse is yet to come

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen is stunned by Geoff's latest move, while Imran tentatively broaches the subject of fostering with Nick and explains how they've put their plans on hold so as not to upset Leanne. Kevin breaks the news to Abi that the twins are emigrating to Australia, while Aggie implores Grace to think twice before ending her relationship with Michael

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya fears that Geoff may win Yasmeen back and Kevin urges Abi to meet up with the twins one last time before they leave for Australia or she will live to regret it. Carla calls in at the factory and admits to Nick that she's bored of pulling pints and feels it's time she got back into the business, while Adam tells Gary it's obvious he's involved in some dodgy deal

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin asks Abi to move in with him and Jack, but, while she is delighted, Seb is angry that she has moved on so quickly from her own kids to Kevin's. Geoff promises Yasmeen that when she comes home, things will be different and he'll seek professional help, while the factory girls are shocked when they realise Carla is once more their boss

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andrea tries to convince Jamie that he must have hit an animal, but Lydia arrives to reveal Moira has been the victim of a hit and run and Jamie struggles to take in the news. Harriet rejects Will's suggestion that they attend a wedding fair. while Amelia is furious when she opens a 'get well soon' card to Dan from Brenda

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie worries about keeping the truth from Kim, but Andrea tells him they must convince her that they are back together for good. Moira lays unconscious in hospital, and Cain talks to the nurse about her condition, anxious he may lose her, while Bob is adamant he can run the cafe while Brenda is away

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nate and Rhona try to calm Cain as Kim threatens to bring down Butlers Farm, while Jamie feels trapped as Andrea holds all the cards. Nicola slyly railroads Jimmy into suggesting that they buy into the cafe, and Belle's heart is broken as her hopes are crushed

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is asked to investigate the theft of a car belonging to a friend of Mullett, but misjudges the grizzled Superintendent's capacity for tact and diplomacy and it is not long before he is taken off the case. Things take a shocking turn when the car finally turns up - with the body of a drug dealer in the boot. Detective drama, starring David Jason, with Melanie Thaw and Bruce Alexander

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour detective Jack Frost investigates a woman's near-fatal shooting. The victim was all set to testify against a notorious drugs baron accused of murdering one of his employees, but the appearance of a contract killer makes her think again - and it appears someone on Jack's team is leaking information. Meanwhile, the station is turned upside down in the hunt for a missing lottery ticket. David Jason, Michael McKell and Bruce Alexander star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper detective accepts an invitation to spend yuletide at the house of an elderly millionaire who seems intent on provoking his money-grubbing sons into murdering him. At first he tries hard to ignore the strained atmosphere and relax - but soon finds himself with a gruesome mystery on his hands. Agatha Christie whodunit, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At the Luxton Deeping Crime Festival, the unveiling of a newly discovered novel by deceased Midsomer crime writer George Summersbee is jeopardised when the manuscript is stolen and local artist Suzie Colebrooke is electrocuted by a booby-trapped roulette wheel. Can new father Barnaby untangle a web of jealousy and obsession to find the killer? With Fiona Dolman, Tamzin Malleson, Liberty Mills, Una Stubbs and Georgia Taylor

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old Susan Webb is desperate find the daughter she gave up for adoption in 1968. At the time, she was suffering with depression, and after being treated with electroconvulsive therapy that severely affected her memory, she was left with no recollections of her child. Meanwhile, Sonia Walsh looks for the mother that abandoned her at the age of three with her younger sister. During her investigations, she discovered her mother had also given up another baby, and she is now determined to make up for the pain of the past. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Observational documentary going behind the scenes of Prince Charles's private estate the Duchy of Cornwall, which he has built up over the past 50 years into a business to support himself, his immediate family and his charities. The first of two parts reveals how the Duchy is run to reflect the prince's personal philosophies, and also explores how the estate supports an ancient way of life on Bodmin Moor but also gives young families the opportunity to embark on a new start in farming

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The master of Gresham College, amateur astronomer Andrew Crompton, is found dead at the Oxford University observatory, and Lewis finds there are numerous suspects, from senior academics to college scouts. Laura Hobson's involvement with a local orchestra helps her give the detective inside information on the complex relationships within the close-knit community, and Hathaway's sharp mind cracks an astronomical conundrum as the team slowly unravels the riddle of the victim's death. Guest starring Sophie Ward (Heartbeat), Diana Quick (Brideshead Revisited) and Robert Hardy (Cornelius Fudge in the Harry Potter films)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The discovery of a body in a shallow grave by the Thames prompts Brooks and Devlin to reopen an eight-year-old murder case, casting doubt on a conviction made by Steel at the time. When the imprisoned man, Luke Slade, is given leave to appeal, he represents himself in court - but what starts as a legal battle soon develops into a personal vendetta. Crime drama, guest starring Iain Glen

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin reopen a 25-year-old case when the bones of a young boy are found buried inside the wall of a communal basement. The victim's former best friend takes part in therapy to revisit her memory of the night he disappeared and reveals a shocking secret, but Phillips and Steel worry her claims will not convince a jury. Guest starring Keith Barron and Colin Salmon, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Animal Orphans
programme.desc: Comedian Paul O'Grady returns with another Animal Orphans, and having previously visited Africa, he now travels farther east to find out about the rehabilitation of orphaned wild animals in the Malaysian part of Borneo. In the first of two parts, he heads for the orang-utan rehabilitation centre in Sepilok, where he discovers how the animals are being threatened by forest fires, learns about the damage that overfishing is doing to sea turtles' natural habitat, and tries to persuade a gibbon to climb a tree - a skill it needs to learn before it can return to the wild

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andrea tries to convince Jamie that he must have hit an animal, but Lydia arrives to reveal Moira has been the victim of a hit and run and Jamie struggles to take in the news. Harriet rejects Will's suggestion that they attend a wedding fair. while Amelia is furious when she opens a 'get well soon' card to Dan from Brenda

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Josie applies for a job as office manager with Mike. Andy tries to get Steve to see that Liz as much to blame as Jim. Fiona throws a grand opening party for her salon

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim returns from Ireland determined to put things right with Liz, but she refuses to see him. Maureen is annoyed when Reg calls off visiting her at the weekend

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is embarrassed by his behaviour towards Tina. Biff wants to know why Dave is rushing into marriage. The Dingles hope to clean up selling Christmas stock in the village

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is not happy in his role as Santa, Kathy and Dave's wedding day arrives, and Vic catches Viv and Terry sharing a romantic moment together

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An informant reveals two local hoodlums are planning to raid a shipment of gold bullion from Haworth Point, prompting Shiner to set up an ambush to catch the would-be thieves. Gina receives a visit from her niece, who has fallen in with a bad crowd back in Manchester, while Liz enlists David to paint the surgery and sitting room - but is less than pleased with the results. Drama, starring Simon Molloy and Tricia Penrose

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is not feeling his usual self, so he goes to see the doctor

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is in for a shock when he finds out who Jean has enlisted to decorate Mike's bedroom

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: The doctor and his wife hire a servant, unaware that the lady in question is his long-lost mother

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A clairvoyant at Whitby Funfair gives a bride-to-be cold feet about her imminent nuptials. Meanwhile, Bellamy is persuaded to cover at the pub, and is almost caught serving drinks after hours, while a new policeman in the village is suspected of being an imposter. Rural drama, starring Ruth Platt, Robert Hands, Mark Jordan and Craig Heaney

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic explodes with rage and drags Terry from the house. The Glovers are shocked to hear about Kathy and Dave's wedding. Mandy is depressed about Dave being no longer available

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jan is still upset about Dave's secret wedding, and Kim finally finds out about Dave's marriage to Kathy. Vic tries to convince the girls he can sort things out with Viv

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim is charged with assaulting Liz, Maureen struggles with maintaining a long-distance relationship with Reg, and Audrey and Mavis make nasty remarks about Rita

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz tells Andy she will never have Jim back, Rita asks Fred to stop sending her meat but agrees to go for a drink with him, and Maureen runs out of petrol on the way to see Reg

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A clairvoyant at Whitby Funfair gives a bride-to-be cold feet about her imminent nuptials. Meanwhile, Bellamy is persuaded to cover at the pub, and is almost caught serving drinks after hours, while a new policeman in the village is suspected of being an imposter. Rural drama, starring Ruth Platt, Robert Hands, Mark Jordan and Craig Heaney

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans directs this episode, set against the backdrop of the first moon landings in 1969. The detective investigates the deaths of an astrophysicist and his girlfriend in a tragic car accident as the rest of the world eagerly awaits news from Apollo 11. When he begins to suspect foul play, Endeavour enlists Thursday's help in getting to the truth, visiting the observatory where the dead man worked and discovering the possibility that he was not the intended victim. The case is complicated further when the dead scientist is revealed to have been part of a mysterious cult-like group

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: An arson attack on a Turkish social club leaves 17 people dead and Brooks, Devlin and Chandler fearing the crime was racially motivated. They uncover a trail of false identities, but face opposition from a respected barrister when they need to remove a key piece of evidence from a suspect's leg. Meanwhile, Steel is forced to confront one of his oldest friends. Guest starring Charles Kay, with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel has a lucky escape while walking home when she narrowly avoids being hit by a car. Shaken, she starts pursuing the driver, who has fled on foot, and the next day she suspects the incident was an attempted murder - and that Nick may be involved. As she tries to deal with the revelation, Janet is left to take charge of the latest case - the gang shooting of a teenager whose friend is a key witness. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack returns to duty after a period of suspension, and finds himself investigating the murder of a man who had been leading a double life. In addition, he also has to deal with the baffling case of a man found dead with his right foot hacked off and replaced by a severed left foot. Drama, starring David Jason and Amanda Root

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: When Sid receives a bonus, the family are quick to remind him how helpful they all are

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is left two large packing cases in an uncle's will - but they come with a terrible curse

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Dr Price and his wife hire a housekeeper, little realising that the woman is his long-lost mother

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Liz's past catches up with her, and Vernon's dreams of wealth go up in smoke. Meanwhile, Merton receives crime-fighting assistance from an unlikely source - two locals dressed as Batman and Robin, who have spotted criminal activity on a derelict farm

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon talks Mandy into dressing up as a Christmas fairy, Frank insists that Kim take a wedding gift to Dave and Kathy, and Biff asks Frank for time off for a honeymoon

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic enjoys watching Viv squirm after her meal with the girls does not go as planned, and she tries to talk to Terry about their future. Tina tells Frank that Steve is jealous

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim cannot stop thinking about Liz and their wasted marriage. Jack arranges a pub outing to a racecourse. Mike tells Kevin he is selling the garage and gives him first refusal

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally is excited at the prospect of Kevin owning his own garage, and Josie is surprised to find how profitable it is. Steve and Andy ask Liz to retract her complaint against Jim

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Liz's past catches up with her, and Vernon's dreams of wealth go up in smoke. Meanwhile, Merton receives crime-fighting assistance from an unlikely source - two locals dressed as Batman and Robin, who have spotted criminal activity on a derelict farm

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A respected gynaecologist is accused of sexually assaulting a patient during a routine examination, but Brooks, Devlin and Chandler struggle to find any firm evidence and the team becomes divided. Crown Prosecutor Alesha Phillips refuses to accept defeat and takes on formidable defence barrister Phyllis Gladstone. Crime drama, guest starring Derek Riddell and Lesley Manville, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Harriet Walter and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Part one of two. The badly burned body of a disabled man turns up in a remote part of Manchester and a few days later another corpse is discovered. The detectives start to realise the victims were not just murdered, but tortured too. They make an arrest, but it becomes clear their investigation has just scratched the surface. Meanwhile, Janet throws her husband out and Rachel's estranged brother turns up on her doorstep. With Lisa Riley and Pippa Haywood

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Downtrodden detective Jack Frost investigates after an unidentified man's body is found on top of a pile of glass at a council recycling centre - and the subsequent autopsy reveals a few surprises. Another case is soon on the cards when an apparent suicide raises suspicions. On the personal front, Frost decides to get in shape and joins a gym, where he falls for the charms of his fitness trainer. Starring David Jason and Claire Cox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old Susan Webb is desperate find the daughter she gave up for adoption in 1968. At the time, she was suffering with depression, and after being treated with electroconvulsive therapy that severely affected her memory, she was left with no recollections of her child. Meanwhile, Sonia Walsh looks for the mother that abandoned her at the age of three with her younger sister. During her investigations, she discovered her mother had also given up another baby, and she is now determined to make up for the pain of the past. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Observational documentary going behind the scenes of Prince Charles's private estate the Duchy of Cornwall, which he has built up over the past 50 years into a business to support himself, his immediate family and his charities. The first of two parts reveals how the Duchy is run to reflect the prince's personal philosophies, and also explores how the estate supports an ancient way of life on Bodmin Moor but also gives young families the opportunity to embark on a new start in farming

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The master of Gresham College, amateur astronomer Andrew Crompton, is found dead at the Oxford University observatory, and Lewis finds there are numerous suspects, from senior academics to college scouts. Laura Hobson's involvement with a local orchestra helps her give the detective inside information on the complex relationships within the close-knit community, and Hathaway's sharp mind cracks an astronomical conundrum as the team slowly unravels the riddle of the victim's death. Guest starring Sophie Ward (Heartbeat), Diana Quick (Brideshead Revisited) and Robert Hardy (Cornelius Fudge in the Harry Potter films)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The discovery of a body in a shallow grave by the Thames prompts Brooks and Devlin to reopen an eight-year-old murder case, casting doubt on a conviction made by Steel at the time. When the imprisoned man, Luke Slade, is given leave to appeal, he represents himself in court - but what starts as a legal battle soon develops into a personal vendetta. Crime drama, guest starring Iain Glen

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin reopen a 25-year-old case when the bones of a young boy are found buried inside the wall of a communal basement. The victim's former best friend takes part in therapy to revisit her memory of the night he disappeared and reveals a shocking secret, but Phillips and Steel worry her claims will not convince a jury. Guest starring Keith Barron and Colin Salmon, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Animal Orphans
programme.desc: Comedian Paul O'Grady returns with another Animal Orphans, and having previously visited Africa, he now travels farther east to find out about the rehabilitation of orphaned wild animals in the Malaysian part of Borneo. In the first of two parts, he heads for the orang-utan rehabilitation centre in Sepilok, where he discovers how the animals are being threatened by forest fires, learns about the damage that overfishing is doing to sea turtles' natural habitat, and tries to persuade a gibbon to climb a tree - a skill it needs to learn before it can return to the wild

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andrea tries to convince Jamie that he must have hit an animal, but Lydia arrives to reveal Moira has been the victim of a hit and run and Jamie struggles to take in the news. Harriet rejects Will's suggestion that they attend a wedding fair. while Amelia is furious when she opens a 'get well soon' card to Dan from Brenda

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie worries about keeping the truth from Kim, but Andrea tells him they must convince her that they are back together for good. Moira lays unconscious in hospital, and Cain talks to the nurse about her condition, anxious he may lose her, while Bob is adamant he can run the cafe while Brenda is away

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie worries about keeping the truth from Kim, but Andrea tells him they must convince her that they are back together for good. Moira lays unconscious in hospital, and Cain talks to the nurse about her condition, anxious he may lose her, while Bob is adamant he can run the cafe while Brenda is away

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nate and Rhona try to calm Cain as Kim threatens to bring down Butlers Farm, while Jamie feels trapped as Andrea holds all the cards. Nicola slyly railroads Jimmy into suggesting that they buy into the cafe, and Belle's heart is broken as her hopes are crushed

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Josie applies for a job as office manager with Mike. Andy tries to get Steve to see that Liz as much to blame as Jim. Fiona throws a grand opening party for her salon

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim returns from Ireland determined to put things right with Liz, but she refuses to see him. Maureen is annoyed when Reg calls off visiting her at the weekend

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is embarrassed by his behaviour towards Tina. Biff wants to know why Dave is rushing into marriage. The Dingles hope to clean up selling Christmas stock in the village

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is not happy in his role as Santa, Kathy and Dave's wedding day arrives, and Vic catches Viv and Terry sharing a romantic moment together

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An informant reveals two local hoodlums are planning to raid a shipment of gold bullion from Haworth Point, prompting Shiner to set up an ambush to catch the would-be thieves. Gina receives a visit from her niece, who has fallen in with a bad crowd back in Manchester, while Liz enlists David to paint the surgery and sitting room - but is less than pleased with the results. Drama, starring Simon Molloy and Tricia Penrose

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is not feeling his usual self, so he goes to see the doctor

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is in for a shock when he finds out who Jean has enlisted to decorate Mike's bedroom

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: The doctor and his wife hire a servant, unaware that the lady in question is his long-lost mother

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A clairvoyant at Whitby Funfair gives a bride-to-be cold feet about her imminent nuptials. Meanwhile, Bellamy is persuaded to cover at the pub, and is almost caught serving drinks after hours, while a new policeman in the village is suspected of being an imposter. Rural drama, starring Ruth Platt, Robert Hands, Mark Jordan and Craig Heaney

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic explodes with rage and drags Terry from the house. The Glovers are shocked to hear about Kathy and Dave's wedding. Mandy is depressed about Dave being no longer available

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jan is still upset about Dave's secret wedding, and Kim finally finds out about Dave's marriage to Kathy. Vic tries to convince the girls he can sort things out with Viv

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim is charged with assaulting Liz, Maureen struggles with maintaining a long-distance relationship with Reg, and Audrey and Mavis make nasty remarks about Rita

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz tells Andy she will never have Jim back, Rita asks Fred to stop sending her meat but agrees to go for a drink with him, and Maureen runs out of petrol on the way to see Reg

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A clairvoyant at Whitby Funfair gives a bride-to-be cold feet about her imminent nuptials.
